when_to_use: radio-friendly smooth jazz, vocal-centric sax/guitar lead, smooth slash chord, full pop-verse-chorus form, 65-115 BPM

defaults.feel: straight, ultra-precise 16ths, no swing, expression carried by lead phrasing not timing

signature_moves: smooth slash chord: Fmaj7/G or Dm7/G in place of a plain G7 (see smooth-jazz-genre.md SS3)
pedal bass: Cmaj9 -> Dm7/C -> Fmaj7/C -> Cmaj9
stepwise bass descent: Cmaj9 -> G/B -> Am11 -> C/G -> Fmaj7
sub-V backdoor resolution: Bbmaj7/C -> Fmaj7/G -> Cmaj9
cadence_options: pre-chorus lift into the smooth slash chord before the chorus downbeat
chorus hook resolves cleanly to the tonic maj9 - avoid a raw dominant 7 landing
avoid: a plain dominant 7th chord where a 9sus4 or smooth slash chord would be smoother (see smooth-jazz-genre.md SS3)
altered/bluesy dissonance - this genre stays consonant and easy on the ear, unlike soul jazz or jazz-funk

hook_archetypes.name: vocal-centric-hook
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a clear, singable 2-4 bar phrase with deliberate rests between statements
hook_archetypes.contour: a scoop/bend into the target note, a held note with controlled vibrato added only at the tail
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Chorus, sax/guitar in a higher register than the verse

hook_archetypes.name: ad-lib-outro-figure
hook_archetypes.rhythm: loose, vocal-style ad-libs around the tonic, not a scalar improvisation
hook_archetypes.contour: short call-and-answer with a keyboard chop, unhurried
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Outro, over the repeating chorus hook as the mix fades

verse.note_lengths: relaxed, lower register, generous space between phrases (1-2 bar rests)
verse.contour: gentle, singable, understated - sets up the chorus lift
verse.placement: on the grid, no drag or push - expression is tonal (bends, grace notes), not timing

pre-chorus.note_lengths: phrases tighten slightly, less space than the verse
pre-chorus.contour: rises gently, tracking the harmony's shift to slash chords

chorus.note_lengths: the fullest, most continuous phrasing of the form - this is the hook
chorus.contour: higher register, the clearest and most memorable melodic statement
chorus.placement: on the grid, confident, controlled vibrato only at note tails

solo.note_lengths: structured and singable - avoid fast/dense technical runs (see smooth-jazz-genre.md SS8)
solo.contour: stays melodic and hook-adjacent rather than harmonically exploratory

drum_skeleton.low_energy: soft cross-stick/rimshot on the backbeat, hi-hat velocity-layered straight 16ths, shaker doubling
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: kick adds light R&B syncopation, hi-hat dynamics widen, shaker steady
drum_skeleton.high_energy: snare switches from rimshot to a layered, compressed full snare at the chorus; pad/synth-strings thicken underneath, still straight and precise - never swung

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: intro is guitar or e-piano solo over a pad, drums thin (shaker/cross-stick only); verse 1 brings the full R&B drum groove and a low-register lead theme; pre-chorus shifts harmony to slash chords and builds tension; chorus lifts the lead register, switches snare to full/layered, adds pad/synth-strings; bridge/solo keeps the melody structured and singable; outro repeats the chorus hook under a fading vocal-style ad-lib

anti_boredom_rules: the melody must leave deliberate space (1-2 bar rests between phrases) - constant note-filling is a genre violation, not a stylistic choice (see smooth-jazz-genre.md SS4)
the chorus must be audibly lifted from the verse - register change + snare timbre change + harmonic thickening, not a copy-pasted repeat
use the smooth slash chord at least once per pass through the form - do not resolve every dominant with a plain V7
the solo section stays structured/singable - no dense technical runs (see melody_phrasing.solo)
the drum grid never swings, at any energy level - straight 16ths throughout (see groove_profile)
